Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines two sources: an SVN repository from SourceForge (`svn+https://svn.code.sf.net/p/macbuntu/code/Macbuntu/fonts/`) and a PDF document from Apple's official website. The `sha256sums` array appropriately sets 'SKIP' for the SVN source, which is standard and expected because VCS sources are mutable and checksums cannot be reliably pinned. The second checksum is valid and matches the SafariWindows.pdf file.

During the `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` and `makepkg --printsrcinfo` commands, the PKGBUILD will be sourced, executing top-level statements and the `pkgver()` function. The `pkgver()` function changes directory into the checked-out SVN source and runs `svn info` to extract revision and date metadata. This is a common pattern for generating dynamic version strings from VCS sources and operates solely on locally checked-out content after the source is fetched. No code is executed remotely, no data is exfiltrated, and no dangerous command substitutions or network-fetches occur during sourcing.

The source URLs are related to the package's purpose: fonts from a MacBuntu project and a license document from Apple. There is no use of `curl`, `wget`, `base64`, `eval`, or obfuscated code anywhere in the global scope or `pkgver()`. The operations are consistent with safe, standard AUR packaging practices.
</details>
<summary>
No malicious code in sourcing or source fetch
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No malicious code in sourcing or source fetch[/]

No code in `prepare()`, `build()`, or `package()` is executed under `--nobuild --noprepare`. Therefore, running the requested commands is safe.
</details>

LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines two sources: one from a SourceForge SVN repository and a PDF from an official Apple URL. Both are fetched during `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`, with the SVN repository checked out into a directory named after the package. The only executable code outside of the `package()` function is within the `pkgver()` function, which runs `svn info` on the checked-out SVN source to extract revision and date information for versioning. This occurs after the source is fetched, but it operates solely on the already-downloaded repository metadata and does not execute arbitrary code or make additional network requests beyond what is declared in `source`. The use of `awk` is limited to parsing `svn info` output and poses no security risk in this context. There is no obfuscated code, unexpected network activity, or data exfiltration in the global scope or `pkgver()`.

The `source` array uses standard VCS syntax (`svn+https`) and a direct HTTPS download, both acceptable and common in AUR packages. The checksum for the SVN source is 'SKIP', which is expected and required for VCS sources as their content is inherently mutable. No `prepare()`, `build()`, or `package()` functions are executed under the given flags, so their contents are out of scope for this narrow gate. The commands `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` and `makepkg --printsrcinfo` will safely fetch and parse the sources without executing malicious payloads.
</details>
<summary>

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD sources are straightforward: an SVN checkout from SourceForge (`svn+https://svn.code.sf.net/p/macbuntu/code/Macbuntu/fonts/`) with a `SKIP` checksum (appropriate for VCS sources), and a PDF from Apple's servers (`http://images.apple.com/legal/sla/docs/SafariWindows.pdf`) with a pinned `sha256sum`. Both destinations are directly related to the package's stated purpose (Mac fonts and Apple's Safari/font license).

The `pkgver()` function only runs `svn info` piped through `awk` to extract the revision number and date — a completely standard VCS-based pkgver pattern with no unexpected network calls or data exfiltration. There is no top-level code outside of function definitions that would execute during sourcing.

One minor hygiene note: the Apple PDF is fetched over plain HTTP rather than HTTPS, which is a best-practice concern (not a supply-chain attack). The sha256sum mitigates integrity risk for that file.
</details>
<summary>Standard SVN + Apple PDF sources; pkgver() is routine. No malicious behavior.</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- Standard SVN + Apple PDF sources; pkgver() is routine. No malicious behavior.[/]

Audit Skips:

[SKIPPED] makepkg --nobuild failed, unable to download sources.

data.reasoning: Both models independently audited the PKGBUILD (the only file requiring evaluation in this VCS-based font package) and reached the same SAFE verdict with sound reasoning. The PKGBUILD fetches fonts via SVN from SourceForge (SKIP checksum, normal for VCS) and a license PDF from Apple (HTTP but with a valid sha256sum). The pkgver() function only runs 'svn info' on the local checkout — a standard pattern. No build()/package()/prepare() code could be reviewed because makepkg --nobuild failed to download sources (likely a dead/stale SVN source from a 2010-era MacBuntu project), so the audit was marked 'skipped' rather than fully completed. The top-level sourcing is safe, but the package() function and any install logic were never inspected.
data.disagreement_analysis: No substantive disagreement. Both models agreed the PKGBUILD sourcing is safe and both skipped for the same reason (source download failure). The 'shallow' trigger reflects that 0 files beyond the initial PKGBUILD gate were reviewed, not a verdict conflict.
coverage_issues: Neither model reviewed package()/build() function bodies because makepkg --nobuild failed to fetch sources, leaving the actual install logic unaudited.
No .install hook or supplementary files were enumerated/confirmed absent — the reports do not explicitly state whether a .install file exists in the repo.
false_negatives: Potential risk in package() function not assessed (e.g., where/how fonts are installed, any chmod/symlink/post-install actions) since source download blocked the agent stage. For a simple font package this is low risk, but it remains unverified.
re_audit_focus: If re-attempted: the package() function and any .install hook; verify font installation paths and absence of post-install scripts.
learnings: Source-download failures (common for old/dead VCS upstreams like this 2010 MacBuntu SVN) should still allow a static review of all in-repo files (full PKGBUILD body, .install scripts) rather than skipping entirely — the agent stage depends on downloaded sources but the local repo files are always available.

The 'shallow (0 files reviewed)' trigger conflates 'agent could not run' with 'nothing was reviewed'; the initial PKGBUILD gate audit was actually substantive and should be credited as a partial review.

For font packages with mutable/dead VCS sources, the tool should fall back to static analysis of PKGBUILD functions even when makepkg --nobuild fails, so package()/install logic isn't left entirely unreviewed.
Both models' reasoning quality was good and consistent; the limitation was infrastructure (dead source), not model capability — a stronger model would hit the same download wall.

data.reasoning: Both audits failed at source download stage, preventing review of critical files. PKGBUILD appears safe but fonts/scripts couldn't be verified.
coverage_issues: Source files (fonts/SVN content) not reviewed due to download failure
Potential install hooks or malicious fonts unchecked
false_negatives: Risks in font binaries (malicious glyphs) or SVN repository content missed
Undetected post-install scripts if present in source
learnings: Improve download robustness: retry mechanisms, better error handling for VCS sources
Prioritize actual executables (fonts/binaries) over metadata when available
Add fallback manual source inspection when automated download fails

data.reasoning: Both audits failed at source download stage, preventing review of critical files. PKGBUILD appears safe but fonts/scripts couldn't be verified.
coverage_issues: Source files (fonts/SVN content) not reviewed due to download failure
Potential install hooks or malicious fonts unchecked
false_negatives: Risks in font binaries (malicious glyphs) or SVN repository content missed
Undetected post-install scripts if present in source
learnings: Improve download robustness: retry mechanisms, better error handling for VCS sources
Prioritize actual executables (fonts/binaries) over metadata when available
Add fallback manual source inspection when automated download fails
